introSection: Classic Coaster is a wooden roller coaster located at Washington State Fair in Puyallup, Washington, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content: The coaster originally opened as a side friction coaster, and used the trains from the Scenic Railway at Oaks Amusement Park. The coaster got an overhaul in 1950, turning it into a normal wooden coaster with trains made by Prior & Church. These trains where purchased from the defunct Giant Dipper at Happyland Park.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: 3 trains with 8 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in a single row for a total of 16 riders per train.

fullText:
Classic Coaster is a wooden roller coaster located at Washington State Fair in Puyallup, Washington, USA.

The coaster originally opened as a side friction coaster, and used the trains from the Scenic Railway at Oaks Amusement Park. The coaster got an overhaul in 1950, turning it into a normal wooden coaster with trains made by Prior & Church. These trains where purchased from the defunct Giant Dipper at Happyland Park.



3 trains with 8 cars per train. Riders are arranged 2 across in a single row for a total of 16 riders per train.
